_meta.description: Golden query → expected-path fixture for CS RAG regression. Each entry asserts that the expected path appears in top_k results (default K=5). High-value entries may also set max_rank to catch ranking drift, not just total misses. Close sibling docs may declare acceptable_paths so assertions stay family-aware as the corpus grows, but high-frequency sibling intents should also earn dedicated queries so prompt-specific swaps stay visible. Queries mix Korean/English to exercise tokenizer + reranker bilingual behavior.

live_readiness_contract.rationale: Stale or corrupt indexes can hide full-mode golden regressions after corpus churn; only a genuinely missing first-run index may skip.

live_readiness_diagnostics.scope_summary: The live readiness hash currently walks all markdown under knowledge/cs, while the index only ingests knowledge/cs/contents.
live_readiness_diagnostics.rebuild_note: Rebuild alone will not clear a stale report when root docs, master-notes, or rag notes change outside the indexed contents tree; a fresh rebuild can flip back to stale immediately if that out-of-index markdown churns again. Beginner-safe fixture checks should remain the trust anchor until the hash scope is aligned.

empty_learning_points_cs_only_parity.description: Beginner transaction cs_only prompts should stay in the same fallback family whether learning_points is omitted as null or provided as an empty list.

stable_full_mode_fixture_queries.description: Deterministic full-mode fixture checks that stay runnable even when the shared live index is stale during concurrent corpus churn. Introductory prompts must keep the primer doc ahead of the deeper companion docs while still surfacing those companion paths nearby.

projection_symptom_only_primer_family_ba.description: Symptom-only projection prompts should stay inside the top read-after-write learning-path family instead of drifting into unrelated docs. This contract is intentionally expansion-friendly: future beginner, intermediate, or advanced bridge docs may join the family, while exact companion assertions are reserved for explicit contrast prompts.

projection_symptom_only_search_regressio.description: A small beginner-safe projection sweep should keep symptom-only prompts anchored to the read-model staleness primer family instead of drifting toward unrelated operational docs.

projection_korean_failover_visibility_co.description: Korean-only beginner contrast prompts about projection freshness versus failover visibility wording should keep the primer first while the failover visibility playbook stays nearby.

projection_korean_stateful_failover_plac.description: Korean-only beginner contrast prompts about projection freshness versus stateful failover placement should keep the primer first while the stateful placement playbook stays nearby.

stateful_failover_beginner_contrast_swee.description: Beginner sibling contrast prompts that mention both global failover and stateful placement should keep the stateful failover placement doc ahead of the generic global failover control-plane sibling, including compact alias-style compare prompts.

korean_only_projection_freshness_primer_.description: Korean-only beginner primer prompts should keep the learner-facing wording in Korean even when the retrieval intent still targets projection freshness primer coverage.

korean_only_failover_visibility_contrast.description: Korean-only beginner failover visibility contrast prompts should avoid falling back to English visibility wording while still anchoring the primer first.

korean_only_stateful_failover_placement_.description: Korean-only beginner stateful failover placement contrast prompts should stay fully learner-facing in Korean while anchoring the projection primer first.

generic_crud_negative_projection_contrac.description: Generic Korean CRUD prompts without freshness cues should bridge beginners toward API or service-layer responsibility primers instead of over-routing to the read-model staleness primer.

stateful workload placement failover control plane 에서 evacuation 과 placement budget 을 어떻게 잡아?
failover 를 처음 배우는데 global traffic failover 랑 stateful workload placement failover control plane 차이를 같이 보고 싶어. regional evacuation 보다 leader placement 랑 placement budget 이 왜 따로 필요한지 큰 그림부터 설명해줘

global failover 랑 stateful failover placement 차이를 초보자 기준으로 같이 보고 싶어. 왜 regional evacuation 만으로는 부족하고 leader placement 랑 placement budget 을 따로 봐야 하는지 큰 그림부터 설명해줘
